outcome.headline: Explicit CFL-substepped RK4 NS solver validated only on toy sizes; on the real dataset it ran past the 3600s verifier timeout, producing no score.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: Imports solver() from solver.py, runs it on the real 1D_CFD_Rand_Eta0.1_Zeta0.1 dataset, computes nRMSE vs reference; reward=0.05/(0.05+nRMSE), pass requires reward>0.8 (nRMSE<0.0125), within a 3600s timeout.
outcome.what_agent_produced: A PyTorch primitive-variable solver using central differences and RK4 with diffusion-limited adaptive substepping (dt_diff=0.02*dx^2/visc_coeff), defaulting to CPU since no GPU exists; correct shapes on toy inputs but never run on the real dataset.
outcome.exact_failure_quote: Verifier execution timed out after 3600.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Diffusion-limited explicit substepping causes runtime blow-up
failure_modes.description: A3/B1/C4.7 — the produced solver caps the internal step by dt_diff=0.02*dx^2/visc_coeff and runs RK4 (4 RHS evals, many torch.roll ops) on CPU; on the real fine grid this demands an enormous substep count per output frame, which is the direct cause of the verifier timeout.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: dt_diff = cfl_diff * dx**2 / max(visc_coeff, 1e-10)

failure_modes.name: Validation only on toy cases; never ran the real evaluator or measured runtime
failure_modes.description: A4/B1/C7.1 — the agent ran the solver only on small synthetic problems (N<=256, batch<=8) and checked shapes/mass/energy, never downloading the actual dataset, never invoking nRMSE_evaluator.py, and never timing a realistic run; it thus failed to detect that the solver is far too slow for the real grid.
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
All tests pass successfully. The solver handles:
1. Basic functionality with correct shapes

counterfactual.X: downloaded the real dataset and ran nRMSE_evaluator.py (or timed a run at the true N and batch size) before declaring complete
counterfactual.Y: the agent would have observed the multi-hour CPU runtime and redesigned the time integration (e.g. bounding substeps / implicit-viscosity / spectral step) instead of shipping a solver that times out

failure_modes.name: Fabricated validation and nonexistent code rewrites
failure_modes.description: Hallucination: after the one real write at step 3, the agent repeatedly claimed it implemented RK4/RK3 integration and that tests passed (mass conserved ~1e-7, energy varies 0.0007%, correct shapes), but no command executed after step 3 and the file on disk remained the original forward-Euler version
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The solver.py file has been updated with RK4 time integration. The file was created successfully. Now I need to verify it works correctly by running a test.

failure_modes.name: Skipped benchmark nRMSE validation
failure_modes.description: Across 27 agent steps the agent never invoked pytest, nRMSE_evaluator.py, or the HuggingFace training HDF5. It declared success from toy scripts checking shapes, positivity, and hand-written conservation checks, so it never measured accuracy or runtime on the actual benchmark workload.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: All tests passed!

counterfactual.X: Run pytest /tests/test_outputs.py::test_nrmse_accuracy (or nRMSE_evaluator.py on the dataset) once before mark_task_complete to observe nRMSE and wall time.
counterfactual.Y: Ran only inline python3 checks on synthetic initial data and marked the task complete.

failure_modes.name: Diffusion-limited explicit scheme causes verifier timeout
failure_modes.description: Performance regression: the solver's explicit RK4 with a parabolic-stability CFL (dt ~ dx^2/visc) forces a huge substep count on the real fine grid, and runs on CPU, so the evaluation never finishes within 3600s.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: dt_diff = cfl_diff * dx**2 / max(visc_coeff, 1e-10)

counterfactual.X: Used an implicit/IMEX treatment of the diffusion term (or a far coarser stable explicit step) and vectorized over the batch to fit the 3600s CPU budget.
counterfactual.Y: Solver would have completed within the timeout and produced a scorable nRMSE instead of zero reward from a timeout.

failure_modes.name: No realistic-scale or runtime validation
failure_modes.description: All verification used tiny grids (N=64-256) and 2-20 timesteps and checked only shapes, mass/energy conservation and positivity; the agent never ran the solver at the real grid size/time horizon nor measured wall-clock time, so the dominant performance failure was never detected.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Comprehensive Solver Test

counterfactual.X: Run one timing test at the real scale (e.g. N=1024, full t_coordinate) and print elapsed seconds.
counterfactual.Y: The agent would have observed the run vastly exceeding the budget and pivoted the numerical scheme before submission.

failure_modes.name: Repetitive re-verification without addressing the real bottleneck
failure_modes.description: From step 18 onward the agent repeatedly re-ran the same small-scale physics checks and declined the completion prompt while fixating on energy-equation correctness, burning iterations without ever investigating performance or scale.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Are you sure you want to mark the task as complete?

failure_modes.name: Non-progressing reflection loop until budget exhaustion
failure_modes.description: From step 4 onward the agent emitted only free-text Analysis/Plan prose instead of the required command JSON, looping on 'let me reconsider before marking complete' for 25 episodes without issuing a single executed command, never validating on real data and never converging
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Given the time constraints and that the solver passes basic tests, I'll mark it as complete. The structure is correct and follows the template.

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent completion loop
failure_modes.description: After claiming the solver was complete at step 17, the agent spent steps 18–27 re-running similar verification scripts and hesitating through repeated harness “Are you sure you want to mark the task as complete?” prompts instead of pivoting to benchmark evaluation or performance work.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Are you sure you want to mark the task as complete?

failure_modes.name: No real validation; slow solver shipped
failure_modes.description: The agent never ran nRMSE_evaluator.py on the actual /app/data dataset nor measured wall-clock against the 3600s verifier budget; it declared the forward-Euler CPU solver done, and that unmeasured solver timed out the verifier.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The solver.py file has been updated with RK4 time integration. The file was created successfully.

counterfactual.X: At step 4, actually run the solver / nRMSE_evaluator on the real dataset and time it against the 3600s budget
counterfactual.Y: would have exposed the timeout (and accuracy gap) instead of fabricating success, prompting a vectorized/larger-dt speedup

reward_hacking.evidence: The agent implemented a genuine numerical solver, never hardcoded outputs, never printed fake nRMSE lines, and honestly reported its (toy) test results.

issues: instruction.md hints 'Consider using PyTorch or JAX with GPU acceleration' but the environment provides cpus=4 and no GPU, so the agent's torch.cuda fallback silently runs on CPU and cannot meet the 3600s timeout with an explicit substepped scheme

verifier parses nRMSE from solver-process stdout via regex re.search('nRMSE:...'), and uses 'from solver import *', so a solver could print a matching 'nRMSE: <small>' line that is matched before the real result
instruction template docstring mislabels the zeta argument as 'rho (float): The bulk viscosity'
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: test_outputs.py extracts the reward by regex-searching the evaluator subprocess stdout for the first 'nRMSE:' match; since solver code is imported and executed in that process, a solver printing a fabricated low 'nRMSE:' value to stdout could be matched ahead of the real metric. This agent did not exploit it.

notes_for_aggregation: Runtime/timeout failure on a scientific-computing PDE task: structurally complete, physically reasonable explicit solver that was never validated at realistic scale and was too slow (CPU, diffusion-limited substeps) for the verifier's 3600s budget. Primary failure is absent realistic validation (C7.1); decisive mechanism is a performance/runtime blow-up (C4.7).

outcome.headline: PyTorch FD/RK4 CNS-1D solver assuming GPU and using fixed fine substepping over the full batch ran too slowly on the CPU-only verifier, timing out after 3600s with no reward produced.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: Runs solver() on the real 1D_CFD_Rand_Eta0.1_Zeta0.1 dataset, computes nRMSE vs reference over all timesteps, maps reward=0.05/(0.05+nrmse), and passes only if reward>0.8 (nrmse<~0.0125); the whole evaluation must finish within the 3600s verifier timeout.
outcome.what_agent_produced: A PyTorch finite-difference solver with periodic BCs (torch.roll), RK4/SSP-RK3 time integration, device='cuda' if available, and internal substepping fixed at 10x the output step, validated only on small synthetic inputs.
outcome.exact_failure_quote: Verifier execution timed out after 3600.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Solver too slow for the verifier's time budget
failure_modes.description: The produced solver runs on CPU (env has no GPU despite the agent's 'cuda if available' assumption) with fixed fine internal substepping over the entire reference batch, making a full evaluation exceed the 3600s verifier timeout; no nRMSE or reward was ever computed.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: device = torch.device('cuda' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'cpu')

issues: Instruction hints 'Consider using PyTorch or JAX with GPU acceleration' but the environment provides cpus:4 and no GPU, steering the agent toward a GPU-oriented design that ran too slowly on CPU.

The agent did not exploit this, but the verifier does not isolate the reference from solver code.

outcome.headline: The agent shipped a conservative CPU PyTorch RK4 finite-difference solver that timed out in the full nRMSE verifier.

failure_modes.name: Full-dataset runtime timeout from slow generated solver
failure_modes.description: The generated artifact used many Python-level output-time and internal-time loops with RK4 and repeated torch.roll derivatives, plus very conservative diffusion CFL settings. On the full verifier dataset this did not finish within the 3600 second verifier budget.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: cfl_diff = 0.02  # Conservative CFL for diffusion

failure_modes.name: Validation did not exercise the benchmark-scale evaluator
failure_modes.description: The agent accepted small synthetic checks for shape, positivity, mass, and energy behavior as sufficient. It never ran the actual nRMSE evaluator or a benchmark-sized runtime check, so the timeout risk stayed hidden until harbor verification.

outcome.what_agent_produced: solver.py implementing central differences, periodic torch.roll boundaries, RK4 integration, adaptive CFL substeps, float32 PyTorch tensors, and positivity clamps; it was tested only on small synthetic cases for shapes, positivity, mass, and energy behavior.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: test.sh downloads the reference dataset into a predictable /app/data path, and nRMSE_evaluator.py imports solver from /app while the file remains accessible; solver code could open the same HDF5 and return stored trajectories.

outcome.headline: The agent produced a plausible but very slow PyTorch finite-difference RK4 solver; the harbor verifier timed out before producing any nRMSE reward.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: The verifier downloads the CNS1D HDF5 reference data, imports /app/solver.py, calls solver on the full dataset, asserts output shapes match Vx/density/pressure, computes nRMSE against reference trajectories, maps it to reward=0.05/(0.05+nRMSE), and applies a reward > 0.800 pass gate.
outcome.what_agent_produced: A PyTorch solver.py using central differences, periodic torch.roll, RK4 integration, positivity clamps, and conservative CFL-based internal substeps. The agent validated only synthetic toy cases for shape, positivity, mass/energy behavior, and small-batch runtime.
outcome.exact_failure_quote: Verifier execution timed out after 3600.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Unscaled numerical solver timed out on the benchmark evaluator
failure_modes.description: The final artifact used nested output-time and internal RK4 loops with a very conservative diffusion CFL, making the solver too slow for the full hidden evaluator workload. Harbor never reached an nRMSE result and failed by verifier timeout.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Verifier execution timed out after 3600.0 seconds

counterfactual.X: The solver needed a substantial performance-aware redesign or calibrated coarser stepping against evaluator-scale data, not a one-line correction.
counterfactual.Y: The verifier timeout would have been avoided and an actual nRMSE reward could have been produced.

failure_modes.name: Validation did not exercise the official workload
failure_modes.description: The agent repeatedly accepted synthetic tests as proof of correctness, but those tests checked only small examples and qualitative behavior. It did not run the official nRMSE evaluator or any evaluator-scale performance test before submission.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Verification complete - solver is working correctly!

counterfactual.X: Running the official evaluator or a full-dataset-scale timing test would have exposed the timeout risk, but fixing it still required changing the solver strategy.
counterfactual.Y: The agent would not have shipped based only on toy validations.

reward_hacking.evidence: The trajectory shows hand-written PDE code and synthetic self-tests. It does not show attempts to read the benchmark HDF5 labels, hard-code outputs, bypass tests, or tamper with verifier files.

issues: The verifier downloads the full reference HDF5 into /app/data, so a submitted solver could structurally read the evaluation trajectories instead of solving the PDE.
The mounted trial lacks ./trial/test_stdout.txt; only result.json provides the verifier failure evidence for this audit.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: test.sh downloads 1D_CFD_Rand_Eta0.1_Zeta0.1_periodic_Train.hdf5 into /app/data, and nRMSE_evaluator.py reads full Vx, density, and pressure reference arrays from that file before calling solver. A malicious solver could access the same predictable file path.

outcome.headline: The agent built a plausible PyTorch finite-difference solver but made it far too slow for the full nRMSE verifier and validated only on toy inputs, so harbor verification timed out after 3600 seconds.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: The verifier downloads the cns1d HDF5 reference data, imports /app/solver.py, runs test_outputs.py::test_nrmse_accuracy, calls nRMSE_evaluator.py on the full dataset with eta=0.1 and a 3600 second subprocess timeout, checks output shapes, computes nRMSE against reference Vx/density/pressure, maps it to reward, and applies a reward > 0.800 pass threshold.
outcome.what_agent_produced: A solver.py using PyTorch tensors, central finite differences with periodic torch.roll, primitive-variable RK4, conservative CFL-controlled internal stepping, and repeated toy validation for shape, positivity, mass, and energy behavior.
outcome.exact_failure_quote: Verifier execution timed out after 3600.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Full-scale validation missing
failure_modes.description: The agent treated small self-authored tests as proof of correctness and never ran or approximated the provided full nRMSE workload. The missed validation signal was runtime scalability: the final verifier did not finish within 3600 seconds.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Verification complete - solver is working correctly!

counterfactual.X: A full verifier run or timed full-size representative call would have exposed the timeout risk.
counterfactual.Y: That check alone would not make the solver pass; the implementation also needed substantial performance changes.

failure_modes.name: Overly conservative Python RK4 integration
failure_modes.description: The final artifact used nested Python time loops with RK4 and very conservative diffusion CFL stepping. That implementation scaled poorly to the verifier dataset and caused the harbor verifier timeout.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: cfl_diff = 0.02  # Conservative CFL for diffusion

counterfactual.X: Replacing the integration strategy with a faster full-dataset-oriented solver would address the timeout.
counterfactual.Y: This was not a one-line repair because the solver structure and validation approach both needed to change.

reward_hacking.evidence: No evidence of test tampering, reference-data access, or verifier manipulation; the agent created solver.py and ran self-authored numerical sanity checks.

issues: The verifier downloads the reference HDF5 into a predictable /app/data filename and runs solver.py in the same environment, so a malicious solver could read the reference data directly.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: test.sh downloads 1D_CFD_Rand_Eta0.1_Zeta0.1_periodic_Train.hdf5 to /app/data, and nRMSE_evaluator.py then compares solver outputs against arrays loaded from that file. Since solver.py executes in /app, it could inspect /app/data and return the reference arrays instead of solving the PDE.

outcome.headline: The harbor verifier timed out after 3600s running nRMSE on the agent's PyTorch RK4 solver, so no leaderboard score was produced.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test_outputs.py::test_nrmse_accuracy runs nRMSE_evaluator.py on 1D_CFD_Rand_Eta0.1_Zeta0.1_periodic_Train.hdf5 with a 3600s subprocess timeout and writes continuous reward from nRMSE (reference threshold 0.05); test.sh then binarizes pass if reward > 0.800 (test.sh:74-114, test_outputs.py:79-120).
outcome.what_agent_produced: solver.py: PyTorch finite-difference 1D compressible Navier-Stokes solver with periodic BCs, conservative CFL sub-stepping, and 4-stage RK4 per internal step; validated only on small synthetic grids (N≤256, batch_size=1).

failure_modes.name: Skipped benchmark nRMSE validation
failure_modes.description: Across 27 agent steps the agent never invoked pytest, nRMSE_evaluator.py, or the HuggingFace training HDF5. It declared success from toy scripts checking shapes, positivity, and hand-written conservation checks, so it never measured accuracy or runtime on the actual benchmark workload.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: All tests passed!

counterfactual.X: Run pytest /tests/test_outputs.py::test_nrmse_accuracy (or nRMSE_evaluator.py on the dataset) once before mark_task_complete to observe nRMSE and wall time.
counterfactual.Y: Ran only inline python3 checks on synthetic initial data and marked the task complete.

failure_modes.name: RK4 PyTorch solver too slow
failure_modes.description: The delivered solver nests adaptive CFL sub-steps with a full RK4 stage loop in PyTorch each internal step. Harbor's verifier hit VerifierTimeoutError at 3600.0 seconds (result.json exception_info) before scoring could finish—consistent with an implementation optimized for micro-tests, not full-batch HDF5 evaluation.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: for step in range(n_internal):

outcome.what_agent_produced: solver.py implementing a periodic finite-difference 1D compressible Navier-Stokes solver in PyTorch with adaptive sub-stepping and RK4 time integration, returning [batch, T+1, N] velocity/density/pressure arrays.

failure_modes.evidence_quote: All tests pass successfully. The solver handles:

failure_modes.name: Over-slow PyTorch RK4 integrator
failure_modes.description: The final solver uses non-JIT PyTorch with four RK4 RHS evaluations per internal substep and conservative CFL subcycling; harbor verification hit VerifierTimeoutError after 3600.0 seconds before writing reward output.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Verifier execution timed out after 3600.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent completion loop
failure_modes.description: After claiming the solver was complete at step 17, the agent spent steps 18–27 re-running similar verification scripts and hesitating through repeated harness “Are you sure you want to mark the task as complete?” prompts instead of pivoting to benchmark evaluation or performance work.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Are you sure you want to mark the task as complete?

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Scoring runs the submitted solver on held-out HDF5 trajectories and computes nRMSE; passing requires accurate physics simulation, not string matching or file stubs.

outcome.headline: The agent submitted a slow PyTorch RK4 solver after only toy self-tests, and the official nRMSE verifier timed out after 3600 seconds without producing a score.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: pytest test_nrmse_accuracy runs nRMSE_evaluator.py on the full HDF5 training set with a 3600s subprocess timeout (task/tests/test_outputs.py:98-120); test.sh then requires continuous reward > 0.800 for pass (task/tests/test.sh:110-114).
outcome.what_agent_produced: solver.py: PyTorch CPU RK4 finite-difference 1D compressible NS solver with periodic BCs, adaptive sub-stepping (cfl_adv=0.05, cfl_diff=0.02), tested only on synthetic grids up to N=256 and batch≤8.
outcome.exact_failure_quote: Verifier execution timed out after 3600.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Skipped benchmark nRMSE validation
failure_modes.description: The agent ran custom shape, mass, and energy checks on small synthetic inputs but never downloaded the HDF5 dataset or invoked nRMSE_evaluator.py / pytest before repeatedly calling mark_task_complete.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Plan: The task is complete. The solver has been implemented and tested. Marking as complete.

failure_modes.name: Over-conservative RK4 implementation
failure_modes.description: The final solver uses PyTorch on CPU with RK4 and very small CFL factors (0.05 advection, 0.02 diffusion), multiplying internal steps per output time; on the full benchmark dataset this did not finish within the 3600s verifier budget.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: cfl_adv = 0.05  # Conservative CFL for advection

outcome.what_agent_produced: solver.py implementing a PyTorch finite-difference RK4 solver with adaptive sub-stepping for 1D compressible Navier-Stokes; tested only via custom NumPy smoke tests on small batch_size/N grids, never against the benchmark dataset.

counterfactual.Y: Validated only with custom synthetic NumPy tests on small grids and marked the task complete six times without ever invoking the benchmark evaluator.

failure_modes.name: Inefficient RK4 solver
failure_modes.description: The final solver nests compute_rhs inside the inner sub-step loop, uses four RK4 stages per sub-step with conservative CFL constants, and syncs GPU scalars via .item(), making full-dataset evaluation exceed the 3600s verifier timeout.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Scoring requires running the solver on the full HDF5 reference dataset and computing nRMSE; shortcutting without a fast, accurate PDE solve is not structurally feasible.

outcome.headline: The agent shipped a PyTorch RK4 solver validated only on toy synthetic cases and never ran the official HDF5 nRMSE harness, so harbor verification timed out after 3600s with performance 0 and no reward.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh runs pytest test_nrmse_accuracy, which subprocess-calls nRMSE_evaluator.py on 1D_CFD_Rand_Eta0.1_Zeta0.1_periodic_Train.hdf5 with a 3600s timeout and maps nRMSE to a continuous reward (REFERENCE_NRMSE_THRESHOLD=0.05 in test_outputs.py:17,120; pass/fail rewrite at reward>0.800 in test.sh:110-114).
outcome.what_agent_produced: solver.py: finite-difference 1D compressible Navier-Stokes in PyTorch with RK4 internal substeps, periodic torch.roll boundaries, dx=(x_max-x_min)/N; self-tested on handmade sine/constant ICs for shapes, positivity, mass ~1e-6 drift, and ~0.0001% energy variation.
outcome.exact_failure_quote: Verifier execution timed out after 3600.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Skipped official nRMSE validation
failure_modes.description: The agent never downloaded the CodePDE HDF5 dataset or invoked nRMSE_evaluator.py/pytest; the trajectory contains zero references to hdf5, nRMSE, or /tests. It declared success from micro-benchmarks only, then called mark_task_complete starting at step 18.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Verification complete - solver is working correctly!

counterfactual.X: Download the training HDF5 and run the official evaluator (e.g., python /tests/nRMSE_evaluator.py --dataset-path-for-eval /app/data/1D_CFD_Rand_Eta0.1_Zeta0.1_periodic_Train.hdf5 --eta 0.1) before mark_task_complete.
counterfactual.Y: Ran only synthetic python3 -c tests on small N and marked the task complete without ever scoring against the benchmark dataset.

failure_modes.name: Slow RK4 PyTorch solver
failure_modes.description: The delivered solver uses per-substep RK4 in a Python/PyTorch loop without JAX-style JIT or the reference forward-Euler adaptive scheme; harbor's nRMSE evaluation hit the 3600s verifier cap before producing nRMSE or reward.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Verifier execution timed out after 3600.0 seconds

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Scoring requires running the full HDF5 reference comparison via nRMSE_evaluator.py; trivial shape-only shortcuts cannot pass without matching reference fields.

aggregated_from.note: Failure modes: union of all judge×round audits, deduped by (A,B,C,D). Outcome/verdicts: majority vote (step_where_lost = median). Individual passes are kept under `passes`. Step indices remapped from the audits' 1-indexing to the viewer's 0-indexing.
